content.problemStatement:
Given an array consisting of n integers, find the number of good subarrays.

Good subarray: A subarray alll, alll+1, alll+2, . . . , arrr−1, arrr is good if the sum of elements of this subarray is equal to the length of this subarray.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer NNN, denoting the length of the array.
The second line of each test case contains n integers AAA1, AAA2, . . . AAAN.


Output Format
Find the number of good subarrays
constraints: The answer fits in a 64‑bit signed integer
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case: first line contains an integer N (1 ≤ N ≤ 100000), the length of the array. Second line contains N space‑separated integers A1, A2, …, AN (0 ≤ Ai ≤ 1000000).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the number of good subarrays, i.e., subarrays whose sum equals their length.

sampleTestCases.explanation: V values are [0,0,0,1]; the value 0 appears three times, giving C(3,2)=3 good subarrays: [1] (pos1), [1,1] (pos1‑2), [1] (pos2).
Only the single‑element subarrays at positions 2 and 4 have sum equal to length (both are 1).
The only subarray has sum 0 but length 1, so it is not good.

solution.approach: For a subarray [l…r] we need sum(l,r)=r‑l+1. Let P[i] be the prefix sum of the first i elements (P[0]=0). The condition becomes P[r]‑P[l‑1]=r‑l+1 ⇒ (P[r]‑r)=(P[l‑1]‑(l‑1)). Define V[i]=P[i]‑i for i=0…N. Every pair of equal V values gives a good subarray. Hence we count frequencies of each V[i] and add C(freq,2) for each distinct value. This can be done in O(N) time using a hash map (or by sorting).
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case (overall O(∑N) for all test cases).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) in the worst case for the hash map storing frequencies of V values.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.buffer.read().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        freq = {0: 1}
        pref = 0
        ans = 0
        for i in range(1, N + 1):
            x = int(next(it))
            pref += x
            v = pref - i
            ans += freq.get(v, 0)
            freq[v] = freq.get(v, 0) + 1
        out.append(str(ans))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
